summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 19 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/extension-registry.mjs:34` — Lazy-init has a double-initialization race: two concurrent async callers both see `_extensions === null` before either resolves, both invoke `loadExtensions`, and the second write silently overwrites the first. Inert for the current sequential task loop but activates on any `Promise.all`-style parallelism. Fix: cache the in-flight Promise — `if (!_extensionsPromise) _extensionsPromise = loadExtensions(cwd); _extensions = await _extensionsPromise`

The three-module decomposition (loader / runner / registry) is clean with correct single-responsibility boundaries and an acyclic dependency graph. The integration point in `run.mjs` is correctly positioned — after brief construction, before dispatch, with three-layer fault containment. All prior `🔴` simplicity findings (including `isValidExtension`, which several reviews claimed was still present) are resolved in the actual code on disk.

The one new finding is a latent double-initialization race in the lazy-init singleton — harmless under today's sequential task loop, but a silent trap if the calling context ever adds concurrency. Flagged as `🟡` for the backlog.

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:4` — 5-second timeout declared and used but has zero test coverage; no test verifies that a slow hook exceeding TIMEOUT_MS returns `null` and increments the failure counter — add a test using fake timers or a hook stub that never resolves within 5s

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:222` — "promptAppend brief integration" tests reproduce the filter + concatenation loop inline instead of calling the actual `run.mjs` path; a change to `run.mjs:1164–1168` (separator, trim condition, type check) won't be caught — label clearly as logic-unit tests or add a test that imports and exercises the real code path

🟡 `.team/features/extension-system/tasks/task-1/handshake.json` — Handshake reflects run_2 state; 2 subsequent commits (`552bb99`, `26ff550`) modified code and test suite without updating it; `test/extension-system.test.mjs` (grew by 93 lines) is not listed as an artifact; handshake is not a reliable record of the actual delivery

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1163` — `promptAppend` fires only for the build phase; `run.mjs:1083` (brainstorm), `1231` (review), `1419/1469` (replan) call `dispatchToAgent()` without the hook; no spec to confirm build-only scope is intentional; carried from all prior PM reviews

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs` — `loadExtensions` tests added (4 cases) but no traversal-attempt test; `extension-loader.mjs:31` traversal guard is the primary security boundary and its blocking behavior is unverified by the test suite

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:236` — brief-append integration tests reproduce the `run.mjs:1164–1168` append loop inline; a change to separator, filter, or trim logic in `run.mjs` will not be caught; carried from prior Tester reviews

**What's resolved since prior PM reviews:** `setExtensions` now has a `NODE_ENV !== "test"` guard (closed 🟡), `fireExtension` has JSDoc documenting the hook contract (partially closes documentation gap), `isValidExtension` is inlined.

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:4` — 5-second timeout is a named design feature with zero tests; add a test using fake timers or a never-resolving hook stub to confirm the timeout branch returns `null` and increments the failure counter

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:235` — "promptAppend brief integration" tests reproduce the `run.mjs:1163–1168` append loop verbatim inline; a separator or filter change in `run.mjs` won't be caught; label these as registry-API unit tests or replace with a test exercising the actual `run.mjs` code path

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1163` — `promptAppend` fires only at the build-phase dispatch site; brainstorm (`1083`), review (`1231`), and replan (`1419/1469`) dispatch without extensions; document build-only intent with a comment and add a test asserting the boundary if deliberate

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:8` — `_failures` Map is session-global but undocumented; a circuit-broken extension is silenced for the full `agt run` process; add a comment documenting intentional session-wide behavior and a test verifying the expected lifespan

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1165` — No size cap on `r.append`; unbounded string passed as positional CLI arg at `run.mjs:290` risks `E2BIG` and unbounded token inflation — add a per-extension truncation cap (e.g. 10 000 chars) with a warning log when truncated

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1163` — Extension output reaches `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` agent with no audit trail; a compromised or buggy extension can inject adversarial instructions that the AI agent acts on with elevated authority — add debug-level logging of each appended string before dispatch

🟡 `bin/lib/extension-loader.mjs:35` — `import(filePath)` executes all module-level code before manifest validation at line 38; a supply-chain-compromised file runs even if it fails the manifest check — document as accepted design constraint with a comment

Three prior warnings are now resolved since run_2: `setExtensions()` is guarded by `NODE_ENV !== "test"` (`extension-registry.mjs:16-18`), the dead `|| full === base` branch is removed from the traversal guard (`extension-loader.mjs:31`), and the hook contract is documented via JSDoc. No new vulnerabilities were introduced. The three carry-forward 🟡 items — size cap, bypassPermissions audit trail, and module-exec-before-validation documentation — are the remaining backlog.

🟡 `test/extension-system.test.mjs:236` — Append loop duplicated inline from `run.mjs:1164–1168`; regression in separator or filter logic in production won't be caught; label as logic-unit tests or add a test exercising the actual `run.mjs` path (carried from run_1 / run_2 backlog)

**Cognitive load** — One carried 🟡: the "promptAppend brief integration" describe block reproduces the append loop inline rather than exercising the real `run.mjs` path. A separator or filter change at `run.mjs:1164–1168` will go undetected.
